name: wsl-clipboard-cd
description: WSL convenience function (cdw) to cd into a Windows folder path copied from Explorer, auto-converting it to WSL form (C:\Users\me to /mnt/c/Users/me). Human shell helper, no agent-facing skill.

methods.name: shell-hook
methods.command: echo wsl-clipboard-cd: the cdw function is written by the per-group shell-alias hook in install.sh (WSL only)

install.notes: WSL-only (Linux side of WSL), no external software and no repo payload. Like gitbash-clipboard-cd's cdc/cdh and claude-code-sessions' cs(), this is a human shell convenience with no SKILL.md. The cdw bash function is written by the per-group shell-alias hook (install_wsl_clipboard_cd_aliases in install.sh, Step 8), which self-gates on WSL (grep microsoft /proc/version) and is a no-op on native Linux, macOS, and Windows Git Bash. Reads the Windows clipboard from WSL via powershell.exe interop. Run install.sh INSIDE WSL to get cdw. Avoid double quotes in check/command — the installer's field extractor truncates at the first quote.

post_install_hints: Open a new WSL shell (or run: source ~/.bashrc) to load cdw.
Usage: in Windows Explorer right-click a folder -> Copy as path (or copy a folder), then in WSL type cdw to cd into its /mnt/c/... equivalent.
Requires powershell.exe interop (default in WSL2) to read the Windows clipboard.
